title: Molten Arabian Macaroni Casserole
description: A rich, molten, festive Middle Eastern dish featuring macaroni, lamb, and a fragrant spice blend simmered in a kazan cauldron.
instructions: Heat the kazan cauldron over medium heat and add olive oil, then sauté the diced onion until translucent.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in ground lamb, breaking it apart, and cook until browned.
Pour in red wine to deglaze the pot, scraping up browned bits, and let it reduce for 2 minutes.
Mix in tomato puree, ground cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and paprika, stirring to coat the meat.
Add vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Fold in the cooked whole wheat macaroni, stirring to combine and allowing it to absorb the sauce for about 5 minutes.
Sprinkle crumbled feta cheese over the top and cover the kazan to let the cheese melt and the casserole become molten.
Cook for an additional 3 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and the flavors meld.
Sprinkle chopped parsley over the top before serving, and enjoy the molten Arabian macaroni casserole.
